page: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features a single shared Intel x86_64 vCPU operating at 2.0 GHz, paired with 5.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes a bundled 90 GB NVMe SSD for local storage and a single complimentary public IPv4 address.

The defining feature of this instance is its fractional GPU allocation, providing 0.0417 of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 2 GB of dedicated VRAM. In benchmark testing, this hardware configuration demonstrates strong performance in lightweight machine learning tasks. Specifically, LLM inference speed for prompt processing using a small 135M model reaches top-tier levels at 9,931.92 tokens per second, while text generation for the same model achieves 454.57 tokens per second. Prompt processing for a medium 7B model also shows strong performance at 211.23 tokens per second.

Conversely, general-purpose compute and memory benchmarks are limited. The single shared CPU core scores poorly in multi-core stress-ng tests and PassMark CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth is also weak, with large-block read and write speeds of 13.71 GB/sec and 10.61 GB/sec respectively. This resource profile represents a cost-efficient entry point for developers needing GPU acceleration for small-scale workloads without the expense of a dedicated physical GPU, though it is poorly suited for heavy database operations or multi-threaded CPU tasks.

This instance is best suited for small-scale LLM inference, lightweight machine learning prototyping, GPU-accelerated development and testing, and basic single-threaded applications that require a small VRAM footprint.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for lightweight accelerated workloads. It features a single shared Intel x86_64 vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 5.0 GB of system memory, and a 90 GB NVMe SSD. The instance is equipped with a fractional slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU, providing 2 GB of VRAM. While general CPU and memory benchmarks are weak, the instance excels in small-scale AI tasks. It achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference for small 135M models, processing prompts at 9,931.92 tokens per second. This profile offers a cost-effective entry point for GPU-accelerated development, prototyping, and small-scale model inference, though it is not suitable for resource-intensive database operations or multi-threaded CPU workloads.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring one shared Intel vCPU, 5 GB of RAM, a 90 GB NVMe SSD, and a slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 2 GB of VRAM for lightweight AI workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-1c-5g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance with one Intel vCPU, 5 GB RAM, and 2 GB VRAM from an NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU.
tagline: A budget-friendly fractional GPU instance delivering accelerated performance for lightweight machine learning inference and prototyping workloads.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A40 GPU with 2 GB of dedicated VRAM
Single shared Intel x86_64 vCPU paired with 5.0 GB of system memory
90 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier performance for small-scale LLM prompt processing and text generation
Cost-efficient entry point for GPU-accelerated development and prototyping
Limited multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth
